theme: The Yardage Book — Strategic Planning Tools

shots.title: Structured roadmap format + schema
shots.notes: Types + 6 compute functions (validate, critical path, parallel, parse, format summary, strategic context). 30 core tests, all passed first run.

shots.title: `slope roadmap` CLI command — validate/review/status/show

hazards.description: TypeScript strict mode rejects SlopeConfig as Record<string, unknown> — interface has no index signature. Required double-cast via unknown.
roadmapCommand is async, so process.exit mock throws become unhandled rejections. Tests needed await/rejects pattern instead of sync toThrow.

shots.notes: 4 subcommands (validate, review, status, show). 16 CLI tests. Architect review includes scope balance, critical path analysis, bottleneck detection, parallelism, fan-in/fan-out.

shots.title: MCP + briefing integration for roadmap
shots.notes: 6 roadmap functions + loadRoadmap helper in MCP registry. formatBriefing gains strategic context section. Graceful degradation when no roadmap. 13 new tests across core + MCP.

shots.title: Documentation + slope init integration
shots.notes: roadmapPath in SlopeConfig (eliminated all prior casts). slope init creates starter roadmap.json. Pre-Tournament Routine in all 3 template variants. 3 new tests.

conditions.description: New subsystem (roadmap) touching all 4 packages — core, CLI, MCP, templates
Session continued from previous context that ran out — required context reconstruction from summary

nutrition.description: Full build+test after every ticket (411 tests at completion)
Commit-per-ticket discipline maintained — 4 feat commits for 4 tickets
S7-4 cleaned up type workarounds from S7-2/S7-3 by adding roadmapPath to config
Tests across all 4 packages — 30 core + 16 CLI + 13 MCP = 59 new tests

nineteenth_hole.how_did_it_feel: Smooth execution. The course-level methodology (vision → roadmap → review) is now a first-class part of SLOPE. 4 tickets, clean linear execution order, no scope surprises.
nineteenth_hole.advice_for_next_player: SlopeConfig interface is strict TypeScript — when adding new optional fields, add them to the interface first (like roadmapPath) to avoid the double-cast-via-unknown pattern. Also, async CLI commands need await/rejects in tests, not sync toThrow.
nineteenth_hole.what_surprised_you: The roadmap review subcommand turned out richer than expected — scope balance, club distribution, bottleneck detection, fan-in/fan-out analysis. All computable from the roadmap JSON without any external tools.
nineteenth_hole.excited_about_next: The roadmap subsystem is foundational for S8+ work. slope roadmap status can now show which sprints are completed/active/blocked, and the briefing automatically includes strategic context.

bunker_locations: TypeScript strict mode: interface-to-Record<string,unknown> cast requires double-cast via unknown
Async CLI commands: process.exit mock in tests needs await/rejects pattern, not sync toThrow
yardage_book_updates: roadmap.ts: cycle detection via DFS with stack tracking, critical path via topological sort + longest path DP
CLI subcommand pattern: switch on args[0], parseArgs helper for --key=value flags
formatBriefing accepts optional roadmap/currentSprint — backward compatible, graceful degradation
MCP sandbox spreads ...core into context — new core exports are automatically available to agents
loadRoadmap fs helper: loads from config.roadmapPath, falls back to default, returns null on missing/invalid
course_management_notes: 2 hazards (TypeScript cast pattern, async test pattern) — both resolved quickly
Tests grew from 395 to 411 (+16 across core, CLI, MCP)
25 files changed, ~2,984 lines added across all packages
Push after each ticket — 4 pushes matching execution order
